text_blocks.text: The United Methodist Church
is organized into conferences. The
highest level is called the General
Conference and is the only organization
which may speak officially for the
church. The General Conference meets
every four years (quadrennium).
Legislative changes are recorded in The
Book of Discipline which is revised
after each General Conference.
Non-legislative resolutions are recorded
in the Book of Resolutions, which is
published after each General Conference,
and expire after eight years unless
passed again by a subsequent session of
General Conference. The last General
Conference was held in Tampa, Florida,
in 2012. The event is currently rotated
between the U.S. jurisdictions of the
church. The 2016 General Conference
will be in Portland, Oregon. Bishops,
Councils, Committees, Boards, Elders, etc.,
are not permitted to speak on behalf
of The United Methodist Church as this
authority is reserved solely for the
General Conference in accordance with
the Book of Discipline.
